title: Morning Briefing — Monday, April 27
content:
FOREX OVERVIEW

The US dollar is mixed heading into a pivotal week of central bank decisions. EUR/USD trades near 1.1730 (+0.22%), recovering from early session lows; Eurozone growth forecasts provide a floor but stagflation concerns cap upside. GBP/USD holds just below 1.3535 (+0.10%), dip-buyers defended the 1.3500 psychological level during the Asian session. USD/JPY slipped to 159.17 (−0.13%) as modest yen strength followed the BOJ's morning release. EUR/JPY consolidates near 186.70, testing nine-day EMA support at 186.50.

  • EUR/USD: 1.1730 — resistance 1.1750; recovering from two-week lows
  • GBP/USD: 1.3530 — breakout pressure building; BoE/Fed divergence in focus
  • USD/JPY: 159.17 — modest yen bid post-BOJ; key level 160.00
  • EUR/JPY: 186.70 — 9-day EMA at 186.50 is the key near-term support
  • USD/CAD: Range-bound ahead of Bank of Canada decision Wednesday
  • AUD/USD / NZD/USD: Commodity-linked pairs cautious on oil volatility and China risk-off

The broader USD trend in 2026 has been bearish amid tariff uncertainty and global growth rebalancing; the DXY is steady but vulnerable to a dovish FOMC surprise this week.

CENTRAL BANKS & POLICY

Bank of Japan — HELD TODAY (0.75%): The BOJ kept its policy rate unchanged at 0.75% this morning as expected and released its quarterly Outlook Report. Markets are scrutinising the report for upward inflation revisions that could signal the next hike. USD/JPY dipped modestly on the release.

Federal Reserve (FOMC) — DECISION WEDNESDAY April 29: The two-day meeting begins tomorrow. The Fed is universally expected to hold at 3.50%–3.75%. Key watch: policy statement language on inflation risk (elevated by the Iran oil shock) vs. growth drag from tariffs. Separately, a Senate Banking Committee vote on Kevin Warsh to replace Jerome Powell as Fed Chair is adding headline risk for USD.

Bank of Canada — DECISION WEDNESDAY: Expected hold as policymakers monitor fallout from the Iran war on oil markets and growth outlook.

ECB & Bank of England — SUPER THURSDAY April 30: Both announce on Thursday alongside US Q1 GDP and Core PCE. Neither is expected to move rates. ECB commentary on Eurozone stagflation and tariff headwinds will drive EUR volatility.

TODAY ECONOMIC CALENDAR

  • BOJ Policy Rate & Outlook Report — Released this morning; held at 0.75% ✓
  • 10:00 AM ET — Consumer Confidence (April) — Watch for tariff/inflation drag on sentiment
  • 10:00 AM ET — Richmond Fed Manufacturing Survey (April)
  • 10:30 AM ET — Dallas Fed Texas Retail Outlook Survey

Week ahead: FOMC decision + US Q1 GDP + Bank of Canada (Wed Apr 29) | ECB + BOE + Core PCE Super Thursday (Apr 30)

INDICES & STOCKS

US equities closed at fresh records into the weekend but futures are pulling back modestly as stalled US-Iran talks weigh on sentiment. S&P 500 futures −0.3% (Friday close: record 7,173.9, +0.1%). Nasdaq 100 futures −0.3% (Friday close: record 24,887, +0.2%). The Dow slipped 63 points in Friday's session.

Asian markets outperformed: Nikkei 225 +1.38% to a record 60,537 as Japan and South Korea equities shrugged off the Iran impasse. European equities opened in positive territory with the Stoxx 600 +0.5%; FTSE and DAX both trading in the green on Iran Strait of Hormuz reopening reports.

COMMODITIES

  • Gold: ~$4,710/oz — extending last week's losses as stalled Iran peace talks reduce safe-haven urgency; $4,700 is key support
  • Silver: ~$75.67/oz (+0.33%) — modest outperformance vs gold
  • WTI Crude: ~$96/bbl — volatile; Strait of Hormuz effectively closed; WTI up ~55% since Iran war began late February
  • Brent Crude: ~$107/bbl — holding above $100 psychological; peaked near $120 at the height of hostilities
  • Natural Gas (US): $2.52/MMBtu (−3.6%) — lowest since October 2024; mild weather forecasts through early May and strong storage injections dragging prices
  • EU Natural Gas: ~€44/MWh — retraced from four-day winning streak on Iran talk optimism

CRYPTO

Crypto is consolidating ahead of macro risk events. Bitcoin (BTC) opened the week at $78,671 before pulling back to ~$77,700. Ethereum (ETH) opened at $2,370, retracing to ~$2,321. Total market cap sits at $2.71 trillion (+1.7%). The Fear & Greed Index jumped to 47 (Neutral) from 33 (Fear) yesterday and 29 last week — a notable sentiment recovery. Watch whether a hold + softer-than-expected FOMC tone on Wednesday reignites crypto risk appetite.

GEOPOLITICS & WORLD EVENTS

US-Iran / Strait of Hormuz: The dominant macro theme. The Strait remains effectively closed, keeping Brent above $107/bbl. Trump extended the ceasefire deadline; Iran has reportedly signaled willingness to reopen the Strait and a second round of talks is expected — the single biggest catalyst watch for oil and risk assets this week. Brent has surged ~55% since the war began in late February (from ~$72 to a peak near $120/bbl).

US Tariffs: The Supreme Court struck down the legal basis for many 2025 tariffs earlier this year, prompting new measures under alternative authorities. Current US tariffs represent the largest tax increase as a % of GDP since 1993 — averaging ~$1,500/household in 2026. Eurozone business sentiment remains cautious on tariff headwinds; EUR upside limited.

US-China: The tech-and-capital flashpoint remains fragile. Any escalation would hit risk assets broadly; watch semiconductor headlines.

Fed Chair Watch: Senate Banking Committee vote on Kevin Warsh (to replace Powell) is a secondary USD catalyst this week — adds uncertainty to the Fed independence narrative.

KEY LEVELS TO WATCH

  • EUR/USD: Support 1.1700 | Resistance 1.1750 / 1.1800 — FOMC + ECB drivers Wednesday/Thursday
  • GBP/USD: Support 1.3500 | Resistance 1.3550–1.3600 — BoE Super Thursday key catalyst
  • USD/JPY: Support 158.50 | Resistance 160.00 — BOJ Outlook tone + FOMC cross-driver
  • EUR/JPY: Support 186.50 (9-day EMA) | Resistance 187.50
  • S&P 500: Record 7,173.9 | Futures −0.3%; 7,100 key intraday support
  • Gold: Support $4,700 | Resistance $4,750 — Iran peace progress = downside risk
  • Brent Crude: $107/bbl current | $100 key psychological floor | Iran talks = vol trigger
  • Bitcoin: Support ~$77,000 | Resistance $79,000–$80,000
  • FOMC (Wed Apr 29): Any dovish surprise or hawkish tilt = high-vol event across all asset classes
